kind: mechanical conformance amendment (pre-ruling; flagged for confirmation in docs/SC-0063-BATCH-RULING-SHEET.md §E-1)

edits.why: The drafter proposed a descriptive token for the deliberately-uncoded collective ('the dead of the household'); the schema's being_id pattern ^B(\d+|\?)$ spells 'uncoded' as B?. The drafter's judgment (entity stays unbound) is preserved — only the spelling conforms. Its original note is intact in fills.json. The exegetical question (B? vs binding to B2/B4/B5) is ruling-sheet item A-4.

Marcia's disclosure ruling (SC-0064, 2026-06-12): bind when the referent set is fixed by the text at that point — 2:20's hammetim = the household's dead, all named by 1:3–1:5 (unambiguous set). Bound per the P02-gold idiom (the scene entry carries the head, B2-Elimelech); the SURFACE withholding the map marks as significant is preserved untouched (referential_form HAMETIM_THE_DEAD; the 2:20b merism slots). Alternative (three entries B2/B4/B5) flagged for her optional refinement.

prompt_followup: fm-drafter-0.1.4 teaches the B? idiom so later drafts conform at source.

regens.what: FM regenerated from the amended map (S1 Significant Absence block added with the blessed wording); the draft's null fill for the former absence gap now rejects by design (the field is deterministic from the map); recorded amendments (B2 binding) re-applied; gaps 95→94.
